text.title: Adobe Firefly Integration Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the adobe-firefly-integration methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Asset-generation pipelines MUST call the Firefly Services REST API. The desktop UI / plugins are forbidden as the integration surface for automation.
rule.rationale: Plugins require a human at the keyboard; pipelines cannot retry / scale.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST pass a brand-compliance pre-filter (forbidden terms + required terms) before submission.
rule.rationale: Firefly will happily generate off-brand output; the filter is the only deterministic guard.

rule.statement: Every generated asset MUST be stored with prompt hash + model version + Adobe Content Credentials manifest.
rule.rationale: Provenance is required for commercial-safe defence and downstream auditability.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST pin to the commercial-safe Firefly model (Firefly Image v3+ — trained on Adobe Stock + CC0). Custom models bypass the safety claim.
rule.rationale: Switching to a custom-trained model voids Adobe's IP indemnity.

rule.statement: Each output MUST carry alt-text generated from the source prompt + manual review.
rule.rationale: AI-generated imagery without alt-text fails WCAG 1.1.1.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
